name: Wickie
heroKicker: I left my desk job to learn how to actually cook.
heroSub: Now I'm a culinary school grad in New York sharing the simple, real food I make at home, so you can make it too. Come cook with me.

aboutParagraphs.text: A couple years ago I was sitting at a corporate desk job wondering what I was doing with my life. So I did the slightly insane thing and left it to go to culinary school in New York City.

Turns out I love it. I spent months learning technique, burning the occasional thing, getting graded on my sauces, and figuring out how to actually cook, and I shared every step of it along the way. You made a scary leap feel a lot less scary.
This is home base now: the recipes I come back to, the things I'm still learning, and whatever I'm cooking lately. Pull up a chair.

recipes.title: Smash Burgers with Special Sauce
recipes.blurb: The crispy-edged smash burgers I make on repeat, with caramelized onions and a pickly sauce that ends up on everything.

recipes.title: Simple Seared Steak
recipes.blurb: Good steak really doesn't need much. A hot pan, real salt, and a quick pan sauce you'll want to drink. School approved.

recipes.title: Summer Veg Plate
recipes.blurb: Light, fresh, and vegetable forward. Proof that simple ingredients at their peak beat fussy every single time.

recipes.title: Weeknight Pasta
recipes.blurb: The five ingredient pasta I lean on when I'm tired but still want something that tastes like I tried.

recipes.title: Roast Chicken & Jammy Veg
recipes.blurb: One pan, crispy skin, soft vegetables underneath. The Sunday dinner I genuinely never get sick of.

recipes.title: The Basics, Done Right
recipes.blurb: Eggs, sauces, knife work. The little techniques from school that quietly make everything else you cook better.

workIntro: I team up with brands I actually use and love, and make warm, honest content that feels like it belongs in my kitchen instead of in an ad. If your product would earn a real spot on my counter, let's talk.
shopIntro: The cookbooks I actually cook from and keep coming back to.
